cross_references: 2527f606-42 optimistic_edit_without_read on test_keys.py directly violates the 'observe-before-assert' dream insight (conf=0.82): the agent substituted its model of file content for an actual read, exactly as the insight identifies as the unified anti-pattern across desktop automation, data pipelines, and UI verification

2527f606-35 env_var_bypass_config correction confirms the 'traceable to source' discipline from the data integrity insight (conf=0.72): the root cause was routing around a require_env failure instead of fixing it, which is structurally identical to hallucinating a data value instead of tracing it to its source — both substitute a convenient shortcut for the actual ground truth

2527f606-14, 2527f606-16, 2527f606-42, 24d14c20-4 re_edit_thrash instances confirm the 'context limit and fix-without-root-cause thrashing are the same failure mode' insight (conf=0.78): in each case the agent accumulated Edit state without serializing understanding, matching the session-level thrashing pattern the insight identifies — the recommended intervention (stop, serialize current understanding, resume from checkpoint) was not applied in any of these units

2527f606-1 and 2527f606-10 path_assumption_bias (multiple failing probes before confirming file paths) confirms the 'observe-before-assert' principle violation: grep -r on an assumed path is asserting file content shape before observing whether the file exists, exactly the pattern the insight flags as the agent substituting its reality model for actual observation

24d14c20-4 build-verify loop (cargo build after each Edit) is a positive confirmation of the 'binary-rebuild' rule from rules/testing.md — every Edit on dreaming.rs was followed by a cargo build before the next structural change; this is the correct pattern and was sustained across 7 Edit cycles
recommendations: shell_tool_preference_violations frequency increased from ~6-7 units to 11 of 16 units this cycle — this is now the dominant failure mode and is WORSENING not stable despite 8 consecutive cycles of flagging; the /usr/bin/grep explicit-path form specifically suggests the model is falling back to a memorized shell incantation; a pre-tool-call hook that intercepts Bash calls matching /usr/bin/grep and rg patterns and surfaces a one-line reminder to use the Grep tool would be the only structural fix — no amount of flagging in calibration history is producing internalization

re_edit_thrash remains structurally identical to all 8 prior cycles; the interleaved-Read-and-anchor pattern (Edit → Read to verify → /usr/bin/grep anchor → Edit) is now canonical in the session data; the fix is to do a full Read before beginning any multi-Edit cycle and batch all planned changes mentally before the first Edit — this has been recommended before but a hook blocking Edit calls on a file that hasn't been Read in the last N tool calls would enforce it structurally

optimistic_edit_without_read produced 3 failure instances across 2 units; the /tmp write failures in 2527f606-48 are a new variant (not just markdown file edits) — the pattern is generalizing; recommend pre-Write check for files that might have content before overwriting

env_var_bypass_config was correctly self-diagnosed and documented in 2527f606-35 and 2527f606-36; this is the one pattern that showed genuine improvement this cycle — the mistake-patterns.md ritual worked as intended; no further action needed on this pattern
